results.title: I'm Done with Discord's AI Features!
results.content:
# I'm Done with Discord's AI Features!
## No Text To Speech
1670000 subscribers
13549 likes

### Description
349098 views
Posted: 19 Jul 2023
AI has been a hot topic everywhere. But Discord... should the AI craze stay away from the platform.

Well I was pretty dumb and welcomed Discord's AI projects with open arms. And after 4 months I've learned my lesson. Discord's AI Clyde chatbot is beyond the definition of "dookie", it's awful. I've seen better Discord bots than Discord's own ai bot.

And the sad truth is that my dislike for Discord's AI aint just about Clyde. AI summaries are more annoying than helpful and both features pose a privacy concern. Automod AI? Don't know what that is cause no one has seen it (except a few that say it's bad). [...] a little too upset I need to pump up the energy because there's another new feature that has potential and I've been so excited about this it's the Auto mod AI it's basically the AI chat bot that moderates your Discord server and flags any weird messages that could be breaking your rules here's a sad thing uh no one's actually really tried it I haven't had my hands on it before uh someone hasn't sent me any images but I did find some messages from someone that did try it please be good please be good please be good please be good please be good it turns out it's it's horribly bad it's complete garbage now honestly I was kind of devastated because this was the only thing that I was actually excited about it could just be so good you wouldn't need to like somehow Gaslight people into [...] good you wouldn't need to like somehow Gaslight people into moderating your Discord server it would just happen naturally and AI would do it it they probably improved it by now since it's been months but my favorite Auto mod AI moment was when it flagged wet as a message because of the rule keep it clean oh yeah Discord AI wet the worst part about these AI features is the fact that Discord employees have to work on these AI features which takes away people from working on the important things like discord's not even finished reporting system that they for some reason are using it still blows my mind makes no goddamn sense and it makes me think why does AI even exist in Discord in the first place business time there are people that have invested in Discord these shareholders are boomers

Eco-Friendly: Every query impacts the planet. discode intelligently selects energy-efficient models and transparently shows you the environmental cost, helping you respect our world.

results.title: Claude is rated "Bad" with 1.6 / 5 on Trustpilot
results.content:
Evaluating 932 reviews, most reviewers were let down by their experience overall. Many customers expressed significant frustration with the subscription model and pricing, feeling that the costs were excessive and that the credit system was unclear or deceptive, leading to unexpected charges and limited usage. People frequently encountered issues with the service, including poor customer support t hat was difficult to reach and unhelpful, often relying on AI chatbots that provided no real solutions. The user experience was largely negative, with many reporting that the AI would frequently cut them off, repeat itself, or hit usage limits quickly, making the paid subscription practically unusable for their tasks. However, some customers also noted that the AI itself was genuinely useful for [...] And worse thing is, it denies that it did that. I can literally see the code it just write (git diff) and execution logs. People want to replace programmer with this AI. Please do.🙏

So the exclusion that Shapes is trying to fall under is being a service provider. But since they're not a service provider, this term is broken, which is why people are getting their bots banned. So hopefully I made it clear why Shapes is getting in trouble for asking for your Discord bot token. It really all has to do with Discord accusing Shapes of training an AI model using message content. And this is the second important part of [...] terms of service and policy that have been violated. We already know about that stuff. But Discord also points out that in the reports, shapes failed to honor users request to delete their API data, which is required under sections three and four of the developer terms of service. As I was investigating shapes myself, I stumbled across people who complained about the same issue. They're talking about how users are opted in by default. Remember how I showed you if you go on the website. The problem though is that there is no opt out button. Like remember these detailed torturous legal terms. If I search for opt in terms of opt out, you can see that you can opt out of website analysis tool for Google data collection. This is only talking about Google Analytics, they have nothing about [...] talking about Google Analytics, they have nothing about opting out. It turns out what this person had to do to opt out is that you have to email Shapes and it took this person at least 2 months just to get a response. And it turns out that Shapes only responded because this person started making noise on the server to get their data off their platform. They had to complain and get angry for shapes to do their bare minimum basic job. And the best part about that whole opt- out situation is that according to a report by Reality Whisper, they point out this forced opt-in and data deletion concerns. results.title: I analyzed 500 Reddit complaints about AI tools. The #1 frustration isn't hallucination. - Indie Hackers
results.content:
Like

 1 Comment

# I analyzed 500 Reddit complaints about AI tools. The #1 frustration isn't hallucination.

by  Chloeally

I've been building in the AI tools space and wanted to understand what actually frustrates people — not what the marketing says, but what users actually complain about.

So I scraped 500 Reddit posts from r/ChatGPT, r/AIAssistants, r/ClaudeAI, and r/artificial over the past 3 months. Here's what I found:

The top complaints, ranked by frequency:

1. "It forgets everything every conversation" — 34% of posts
2. "It's too expensive for what it does" — 22%
3. "I have to re-explain my context every time" — 19%
4. "The output quality is inconsistent" — 15%
5. "It can't do multi-step tasks reliably" — 10%

The #1 complaint isn't hallucination. It's memory. [...] The #1 complaint isn't hallucination. It's memory.

People aren't frustrated that AI gets things wrong. They're frustrated that AI never gets to know them. Every session starts from zero. Every workflow has to be re-explained. Every preference has to be re-stated.

The math is brutal: if you spend 15 minutes per day re-explaining context to your AI, that's 91 hours per year. For a solo founder, that's 2+ full work weeks — gone.

I've been working on this problem with AllyHub — an agent that actually remembers across sessions and builds on what it already knows. Happy to share more if anyone's interested.

What's your biggest frustration with current AI tools?

Avatar for user @Chloeally   Chloeally
